FROM node:22-alpine

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --production

COPY src/ ./src/

EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]

content.text: Build succeeded. Now let's run it briefly to verify startup:

input.command: docker run --rm -d --name spectopus-test -p 3001:3000 spectopus:test && sleep 3 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3001/ ; docker logs spectopus-test ; docker stop spectopus-test
input.description: Run container, check HTTP response and logs, then stop it

content.text:
Build and run both work correctly:

- **Build**: `npm ci --production` installs 353 packages, 0 vulnerabilities, image layers cached cleanly.
- **Runtime**: Container starts, logs `Spectopus listening on port 3000`, and responds to HTTP (404 on `/` is expected — no route defined there).

The Dockerfile looks good. One minor note: `npm ci --production` triggers a deprecation warning (`Use --omit=dev instead`). You could update line 6 to `RUN npm ci --omit=dev` to silence it, but it's cosmetic.
